title: Gaillardia suavis
intro: Gaillardia suavis, commonly known as Indian blanket or firewheel, is a flowering plant in the aster family native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The health benefits of Gaillardia suavis include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Gaillardia suavis is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as in topical creams and salves, to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
